title: BEM-916 curator response
objective:
Create file governance/audit/mailbox/from_curator/BEM916_UNIFIED_SYSTEM_IMPROVEMENT_PROTOCOL_RESPONSE.md with content # BEM-916 UNIFIED SYSTEM IMPROVEMENT PROTOCOL RESPONSE
Status: APPROVED_WITH_NOTES
Findings:
- Worker confirms the operator correction is valid.
- BEM-915 must not remain a standalone Telegram protocol.
- Telegram Operator Interface must be incorporated into the previous BEM-914 system improvement protocol as a dedicated phase and mechanism.
- Unified protocol must keep all BEM-914 phases and add Telegram as an integrated operator-facing layer.
Evidence:
- governance/audit/mailbox/to_curator/BEM916_UNIFIED_SYSTEM_IMPROVEMENT_PROTOCOL_REQUEST.md
- governance/audit/internal/results/BEM916_WORKER_UNIFIED_SYSTEM_IMPROVEMENT_PROTOCOL_REVIEW.md
- governance/reports/BEM914_SYSTEM_IMPROVEMENT_PROTOCOL.md
- governance/reports/BEM915_TELEGRAM_OPERATOR_INTERFACE_PROTOCOL.md
Agreed changes:
- Prepare unified protocol DOCX/ZIP.
- Include BEM-914 phases: proof hardening, dispatch lifecycle, object execution, channel/event log, architecture canon, Claude re-audit.
- Add Telegram Operator Interface as an integrated phase: task intake, commands, envelope, routing to Curator/Director/General Director/Worker, role-based reports, receipts, correlation IDs, errors, E2E selftest.
- State clearly that Telegram uses policy gate, dispatch queue, managed channel, proof policy and role hierarchy; it must not bypass governance.
Operator gates:
- bot token/secrets, production webhook, issue #31 writes, schedule trigger, always-on daemon, real pilot/client/scope/metrics and LLM-agent runtime model.
Recommendation:
- Submit unified protocol to operator for review.
No issue comments.
